MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-28-2023 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR QUIET TITLE, DECLARATORY RELIEF, AND PARTITION BY SALE IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO CLEARLY INDICATE THE PERSON/ENTITIES WITH ADVERSE CLAIMS TO THE TITLE. IF PLAINTIFF NO LONGER CLAIMS THAT THE ESTATE OF BHIDE ASSERTED ANY ADVERSE CLAIMS TO THE TITLE, PLAINTIFF MUST SO CLEARLY ALLEGE (AT LEAST BY REMOVING REFERENCE TO THE ESTATE FROM THE COMPLAINT). MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. ANY CHALLENGES TO THE SUFFICIENCY OF ALLEGATIONS MUST BE MADE BY A DEMURRER OR A MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE COURT NOTES THAT THE BASIS FOR PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM TO 50% INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY BELONGING TO BHIDE PER THE GRANT DEED ATTACHED TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS NOT SUFFICIENTLY STATED (I.E. FACTS SHOWING EXEMPTION FROM STATUTE OF FRAUDS) AND URGES THE PLAINTIFF TO ADDRESS THIS ISSUE ON THE AMENDMENT TO AVOID FURTHER CHALLENGE TO THE PLEADINGS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PROPERLY ALLEGE ALL ELEMENTS OF THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR QUIET TITLE; ATTACH THE DEED WHICH FORMS THE BASIS OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM (FAC 3:28-4:1); PROPERLY NAME THE ESTATE AS THE DEFENDANT IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS A DECLARATION AGAINST THE ESTATE'S CLAIMS OF OWNERSHIP. THE COURT NOTES THAT PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PARTICIPATE IN THE MANDATORY MEET AND CONFER PROCESS. NO FURTHER CHALLENGES TO PLEADINGS WILL BE HEARD BY THE COURT ABSENT PLAINTIFF'S FULL PARTICIPATION IN THE MEET AND CONFER PROCESS. IF PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PARTICIPATE, DEFENDANT SHALL FILE DECLARATIONS REGARDING EXTENSION UNTIL SUCH TIME THAT PLAINTIFF COMPLIES WITH ITS STATUTORY OBLIGATIONS. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2023 Continued To Jun-07-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Strike And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint For Quiet Title, Declaratory Relief, And Partition By Sale
Off Calendar Feb-28-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FOR QUIET TITLE, DECLARATORY RELIEF, AND PARTITION BY SALE is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to clearly indicate the person/entities with adverse claims to the title. If Plaintiff no longer claims that the estate of Bhide asserted any adverse claims to the title, Plaintiff must so clearly allege (at least by removing reference to the estate from the complaint). Motion is otherwise denied. Any challenges to the sufficiency of allegations must be made by a demurrer or a motion for judgment on the pleadings. The Court notes that the basis for Plaintiff's claim to 50% interest in the property belonging to Bhide per the Grant Deed attached to the Second Amended Complaint is not sufficiently stated (i.e. facts showing exemption from Statute of Frauds) and urges the Plaintiff to address this issue on the amendment to avoid further challenge to the pleadings.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Continued To Jan-04-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-12-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly allege all elements of the cause of action for quiet title; attach the deed which forms the basis of Plaintiff's claim (FAC 3:28-4:1); properly name the estate as the defendant if Plaintiff seeks a declaration against the estate's claims of ownership. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to participate in the mandatory meet and confer process. No further challenges to pleadings will be heard by the Court absent Plaintiff's full participation in the meet and confer process. If Plaintiff fails to participate, Defendant shall file declarations regarding extension until such time that Plaintiff complies with its statutory obligations.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)
